skill.name: longbridge-market-data
skill.description:
Real-time quotes, K-line charts, order book, trade ticks, intraday capital flow, market sentiment temperature, trading session schedule, security lists, exchange rates, and IPO calendar for HK/US/A-share/SG via Longbridge. Also covers ADR premium and FX carry frameworks.
Triggers: "股价", "行情", "K线", "走势", "盘口", "资金流", "市场温度", "汇率", "IPO", "打新", "隔夜股", "ADR溢价", "外汇套息", "K線", "盤口", "資金流", "市場溫度", "匯率", "ADR溢價", "外匯套息", "现在多少钱", "多少钱", "stock price", "quote", "kline", "chart", "depth", "orderbook", "capital flow", "market sentiment", "exchange rate", "IPO calendar", "security list", "ADR premium", "fx carry", "market open", "trading hours", "开市", "溢价", "NVDA.US", "700.HK", "600519.SH", "股價", "走勢", "開盤", "今天開市"
skill.summary: Real-time and historical market data for HK, US, A-share, and Singapore via the Longbridge CLI, covering quotes, K-line charts, order book depth, capital flow, and IPO calendar.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: The skill is a prompt-only markdown documentation set (19 files, 995 lines) that instructs the LLM to invoke the Longbridge CLI for market data. All 331 'external_commands' and 37 'weak cryptographic algorithm' static findings are false positives: backticks in markdown are code formatting (e.g. `longbridge quote`), and hash references are documentation context, not executable code. The one 'hardcoded URL' is a legitimate GitHub project link. No scripts, no code execution, no data exfiltration paths.

low_findings.title: False positive: backtick patterns in markdown
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 331 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. All are markdown code formatting (e.g. `longbridge quote`, `NVDA.US`). No actual code execution paths exist. Confidence 0.95 false positive.

low_findings.title: False positive: hardcoded URL
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged a URL at references/quote.md:75. The URL is https://github.com/longportapp/longbridge-terminal — a legitimate, public project link for installation instructions. Confidence 0.98 false positive.

content.user_title: Get real-time market data via Longbridge CLI
content.value_statement: Access real-time quotes, K-line charts, order book depth, and IPO calendars for global markets directly in your AI assistant. Eliminates the need to switch between multiple trading terminals for basic market data queries.
actual_capabilities: Fetch real-time stock quotes for HK, US, A-share, and Singapore markets
Retrieve K-line/candlestick OHLCV data with configurable time ranges
Query order book depth (Level 2 bid/ask ladder) and broker queue data
Access intraday capital flow distribution and market sentiment temperature index
Look up trading session schedules, market open/close status, and IPO calendar
Calculate cross-market metrics like A/H premium ratio and ADR premium framework
limitations: Requires the longbridge-terminal CLI installed locally, or MCP server configured
IPO orders and profit-loss queries require Trade permission login
Active WebSocket subscription data requires an authenticated session token
Symbol format must follow <CODE>.<MARKET> convention; ambiguous names require user clarification

use_cases.title: Quick price check during market hours
use_cases.description: Ask for the current price of any stock across HK, US, A-share, or Singapore markets and get an instant quote with change percentage.

use_cases.title: Technical analysis data retrieval
use_cases.description: Pull K-line candlestick data or intraday minute charts for technical analysis workflows including support/resistance and trend identification.

use_cases.title: IPO subscription and calendar lookup
use_cases.description: Browse upcoming IPO calendars across markets, check subscription status, and review historical IPO profit/loss for Hong Kong and US listings.

prompt_templates.title: Single stock price check
prompt_templates.prompt: What is the current price of NVDA.US?

prompt_templates.title: Multi-symbol quote batch
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me real-time quotes for 700.HK, AAPL.US, and 600519.SH with change percentage and volume.

prompt_templates.title: K-line data with date range
prompt_templates.prompt: Get daily K-line data for TSLA.US from 2026-01-01 to 2026-06-01.

prompt_templates.title: Order book depth analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the Level 2 order book depth for 700.HK including top 10 bid and ask levels with broker queue.

output_examples.input: What is the current price of NVDA.US?

output: Date       | Open    | High    | Low     | Close   | Volume
Source: Longbridge Securities / 长桥证券

output_examples.input: What is the A/H premium for 600519.SH?

best_practices: Always run `longbridge --help` first to discover available subcommands and flags — the CLI evolves, so never hard-code flag names in prompts
Normalize stock symbols to <CODE>.<MARKET> format (e.g. NVDA.US, 700.HK) before calling any subcommand to avoid ambiguity errors
Use parallel subcommand calls when the agent runtime supports it to speed up multi-symbol queries across quote, static, and calc-index
anti_patterns: Do not hard-code specific longbridge subcommand names or flags in your prompts — use --help discovery at runtime instead
Do not guess the market suffix for a ticker; if ambiguous (e.g. Chinese company name), ask the user to confirm the market
Do not silently retry on stderr errors; surface the error verbatim to the user for transparent troubleshooting

faq.question: What is the <CODE>.<MARKET> symbol format?
faq.answer: Uppercase ticker plus .US for US stocks, 4-digit number plus .HK for Hong Kong, 6-digit starting with 60 plus .SH for Shanghai, 6-digit starting with 00 or 30 plus .SZ for Shenzhen, and ticker plus .SG for Singapore. Examples: NVDA.US, 700.HK, 600519.SH, D05.SG.

faq.question: Do I need to log in to use market data?
faq.answer: Most public market data commands (quote, kline, depth, trades, market-temp, etc.) do not require login. Only IPO orders/profit-loss, active WebSocket subscriptions, and account-specific queries require authentication via `longbridge auth login`.

faq.question: What happens if longbridge CLI is not installed?
faq.answer: If the shell returns `command not found: longbridge`, the skill falls back to the Longbridge MCP server if configured. Otherwise it tells you to install longbridge-terminal via brew: `brew tap longbridge/tap && brew install longbridge/tap/longbridge-terminal`.

faq.question: How do I get intraday minute-by-minute data?
faq.answer: Use the `intraday` subcommand with the target symbol. Run `longbridge intraday --help` to see available flags for session selection and output format.

faq.question: Can I check IPO subscription status?
faq.answer: Yes. Use `longbridge ipo calendar` for upcoming IPOs, `longbridge ipo subscriptions` for HK subscriptions, and `longbridge ipo us-subscriptions` for US markets. Historical profit/loss requires Trade permission login.

faq.question: What is the A/H premium and how is it calculated?
faq.answer: A/H premium is the percentage difference between an A-share price (Shanghai/Shenzhen) and its H-share price (Hong Kong) for dual-listed companies, adjusted for exchange rate. Positive means A-shares trade at a premium. Use the `ah-premium` subcommand to fetch this data.
